Prior phase 7 detail: (guest-meeting-coturn-pc-connection) — COMPLETE 2026-05-21. Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 2 files / 13 insertions + 1 deletion in dev compose + 1 new typed lesson). SCOPE: discharge the queued ticket spun out by (disconnect-reason-dual-meaning) — investigate why `room.connect()` fails with "could not establish pc connection" on the guest path despite (coturn-config-fix) `75ab34f` having fixed coturn's startup crash. PRE-FLIGHT discovery (static config audit + cross-reference of [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] + [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]]): coturn runs cleanly now; LiveKit env-var rename is canonical; client passes no rtcConfig/iceServers (verified `use-meeting-room.ts:102`). The MISSING WIRING is between LiveKit dev compose and the host browser, not between coturn and LiveKit (which on localhost dev are independent — coturn isn't needed when --node-ip=127.0.0.1 + 1:1 UDP mapping make direct UDP work). DIAGNOSIS (static): three defects in `deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.media.yml`. (a) `--dev` autodetects nodeIP as the Docker bridge interface (172.x.x.x) — unreachable from the host browser's network namespace. ICE candidates advertise that bridge IP → PC connection fails. (b) UDP port mapping was non-1:1: `${LIVEKIT_TURN_UDP_START}:7882/udp`. LiveKit binds + advertises 7882 (container) but the host only opens 43537 → even if the IP were reachable, port doesn't match. (c) Coturn isn't wired as a `RTC.TURN_SERVERS` fallback — not needed for localhost dev, deferred for stage/prod follow-up. FIX (3 lines added + 1 modified): added `--node-ip=127.0.0.1` to LiveKit dev `command:`; added `--udp-port=${LIVEKIT_TURN_UDP_START}` (pins LiveKit to bind+advertise the same port as the host mapping); changed UDP port mapping from `${LIVEKIT_TURN_UDP_START}:7882/udp` to `${LIVEKIT_TURN_UDP_START}:${LIVEKIT_TURN_UDP_START}/udp` (1:1). FLAG TRAP DISCOVERED during reproduction: first attempt used `--rtc-port-range-start=…` + `--rtc-port-range-end=…` (the syntax stage compose uses) — `livekit-server:1.11.0` rejected these with `flag provided but not defined: -rtc-port-range-start`, exit 0 → infinite restart loop (caught by `head` of `docker logs`). Correct flag is the singular `--udp-port`. STAGE+PROD compose files have the same broken flag pair AND no --node-ip — same class of bug, intentionally deferred to (guest-meeting-livekit-turn-stage-prod) follow-up. VALIDATION: `docker compose ... up -d --no-deps --force-recreate livekit` clean (single restart; nodeIP="127.0.0.1", rtc.portUDP={Start:43537,End:0} confirmed in startup log; port mapping `0.0.0.0:43537->43537/udp` published). SESSIONLESS PLAYWRIGHT SMOKE (native pnpm dev with `set -a; source .env.dev; set +a; pnpm dev`, port 43512; reused test data meeting `cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6` / token `a495c303-3d1a-47f7-b99c-a055319f6b74`; participant `Smoke-CoturnPC`): full console timeline shows TWO connect attempts. **Attempt 1** aborts pre-signal with empty roomID/participantID — React StrictMode running the useEffect cleanup synchronously during the first effect's connect-in-flight. **Attempt 2** runs `signal connecting → signal connected → connected to Livekit Server` with roomID=RM_dJzZnLpVv7WP, participantID=PA_F7ARrjwhcPTS populated; LiveKit server-side logs confirm joinDuration=63.3ms and ping/pong flowing. Page snapshot after 12s: meeting room renders with heading "Meeting", "1 participant · 01:55" counter ticking, Smoke-CoturnPC participant tile visible, full mic/camera/screen-share/leave toolbar — NO "Could not join — could not establish pc connection" failed-state CTA. **End-to-end guest meeting signal path WORKS.** TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — investigation done first; root cause empirically identified; minimal-blast-radius dev-only fix applied; smoke confirms working state. Stage 2 quality PASS — single-file diff (3 added lines + 1 modified), comprehensive comment explaining the latent Docker-WebRTC trap with cross-link to [[livekit-client-initiated-dual-meaning]], no `any` types, no scope creep into stage/prod (deferred per pre-declared plan), conventional commit ahead. SECURITY (security.md): no behavior change beyond LiveKit binding 127.0.0.1 for advertisement (dev environment only; production uses Traefik + public domain per V27). MEDIA VERIFICATION GAP (intentionally documented): Playwright headless Chrome has no real camera/mic (`Requested device not found` fires on `enableCameraAndMicrophone()`), so media UDP flow over the new 1:1-mapped port range is NOT empirically verified by this smoke. The compose change is still correct defensive hardening — without it, ICE candidates point at an unreachable bridge IP regardless of whether media tracks publish. HEURISTIC REFINEMENT: today's smoke disproves part of yesterday's (disconnect-reason-dual-meaning) heuristic. The "empty roomID/participantID in abort log = transport failure" rule is too sweeping — StrictMode cleanup mid-connect produces the same signature. Refined heuristic is documented in the new typed lesson: look for a SECOND signal-connecting message; if attempt 2 succeeds with populated IDs, attempt 1's abort was StrictMode (benign, dev-only); if no second attempt OR also aborts pre-signal, transport failure is genuine. The (disconnect-reason-dual-meaning) helper-text in `describeDisconnectReason` isn't actively misleading (it surfaces the ambiguity correctly) but the diagnosis heuristic in the description text needs refinement in a small follow-up; not done in this branch (zero source-code changes to that helper this ticket). 1 NEW TYPED LESSON: 🔴 [[livekit-dev-docker-node-ip-port-mismatch]] documenting (a) the Docker-WebRTC node-IP trap, (b) the --rtc-port-range-* vs --udp-port flag rename, (c) the refined StrictMode disambiguation heuristic, (d) the verification gap re: real-camera media flow, (e) cross-link to [[livekit-client-initiated-dual-meaning]] superseding its over-broad heuristic. SKIPPED skill auto-suggestions (verification, nextjs, next-cache-components) per Rule 28 — pure Docker compose config edit, no Next.js framework guidance applicable. END STATE: guest meeting signal path works end-to-end on dev; full meeting room UI renders; participant counter ticks; ready for multi-user/real-media smoke whenever the rig is set up. Closes (guest-meeting-coturn-pc-connection) from queue.

PRIOR_PHASE_3: (disconnect-reason-dual-meaning) — COMPLETE 2026-05-21, squash-merged to main as `4d1cdae` (implementation branch was `feat/disconnect-reason-dual-meaning`, branched from main tip `a5dc1d9`, intermediate commit `93f64de`, branch deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 files / governance + helper + test). Corrective fix-up ticket spun out of the failed (guest-meeting-loader-memo-stability) verification smoke. SCOPE: (a) attempted `(guest-meeting-loader-memo-stability)` ABANDONED — wrapped `guestCredentials` in `useMemo` on branch `feat/guest-meeting-loader-memo-stability`, ran sessionless Playwright smoke against `/app/meeting/{id}?guest=1`. Result disproved the prior ticket's verdict: console still showed `CLIENT_INITIATED`, page UI rendered "Could not join — could not establish pc connection" with the failed-state "Return to home" CTA. LiveKit's own preceding log `Abort connection attempt due to user initiated disconnect {room: , roomID: undefined, participant: , participantID: }` has EMPTY roomID + EMPTY participantID — proving the room was NEVER fully connected. The CLIENT_INITIATED is LiveKit's internal abort path on a failed `room.connect()`, NOT our useEffect cleanup. The real root cause is hypothesis (a) — coturn/ICE/TURN failure on the guest path. The `useMemo` change had no measurable user impact and the branch was deleted. (b) `describeDisconnectReason` helper UPDATED — CLIENT_INITIATED's `description` now explicitly surfaces the DUAL MEANING: "Fires for BOTH (a) explicit room.disconnect() (our useEffect cleanup or hangup) AND (b) LiveKit's internal abort when connect() fails. Disambiguate by checking the preceding LiveKit log line: empty roomID/participantID = never-connected = transport/ICE failure surfacing through CLIENT_INITIATED. Populated = real cleanup." (c) Test updated to assert the new disambiguation language (matches AMBIGUOUS + connect\(\) fails|ICE|transport + cleanup|hangup|unstable refs + roomID|participantID). 9/9 helper tests pass post-edit. (d) NEW 🔴 lesson `[[livekit-client-initiated-dual-meaning]]` written documenting the gotcha + recognition heuristic + wider learning (smoke verification of console output is NOT smoke verification of user-visible behavior — always snapshot the page state too). (e) NEW QUEUE TICKET filed: `(guest-meeting-coturn-pc-connection)` — investigate why `room.connect()` fails with "could not establish pc connection" on the guest path, despite `(coturn-config-fix)` `75ab34f` having fixed the auth-secret + deprecated-flag issues. Likely candidates: coturn's STUN works (verified at the end of coturn-config-fix) but TURN relay may not — host-side hosts work because they use STUN-only direct-peer paths, guests over a more restrictive network may need TURN relay; OR the LiveKit server's `rtc.turn_servers` config doesn't include our coturn instance; OR LiveKit auto-served TURN credentials aren't reaching the guest browser; OR the coturn realm mismatches the LiveKit-issued credentials. (f) prior ticket `(guest-meeting-livekit-peer-disconnect)` `c585e52` is NOT being reverted — the instrumentation itself is sound and the captured CLIENT_INITIATED is the correct enum value; only my INTERPRETATION was wrong. The helper update + lesson make the next smoke self-correcting. VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run helper file 9/9 ✓ (full suite to run). Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — corrective scope met. Stage 2 quality PASS — TDD-evidence retained for the helper, no scope creep. SECURITY: no behavior change beyond the helper's description text. END STATE: future smokes against any LiveKit Disconnected event have the disambiguation guidance baked into the helper output + in lessons.md.

PRIOR_PHASE_3: (guest-meeting-livekit-peer-disconnect) — COMPLETE 2026-05-21, instrumentation phase shipped and squash-merged to main as `c585e52` (implementation branch was `feat/guest-meeting-livekit-peer-disconnect`, branched from main tip `c1c2775`, intermediate commits `dbaa90c` source + `cd3e051` governance, branch deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 files / 334 insertions / 2 deletions). SCOPE: Tier 1 direct follow-up to `(meeting-room-guest-disconnect-redirect)` `24101c6`. That ticket gated the SYMPTOM (sessionless guests no longer bounce to /login on meeting end). This ticket instruments the ROOT CAUSE — the ~3s post-signal-connect LiveKit peer disconnect on guest path — so the next session can confirm empirically which of the three pre-ranked hypotheses is correct: (a) coturn/ICE/TURN failure despite `75ab34f` container fix (most likely given timing); (b) StrictMode duplicate-identity collision; (c) `useMeetingRoom` effect re-run from unstable `guestCredentials` object literal at `guest-meeting-room-loader.tsx:91-94`. FIX (3 files, 2 new, 1 modified): (a) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/livekit/disconnect-reason.ts` (+207) — pure helper `describeDisconnectReason(reason): {reason, label, hypothesis, description}` exhaustively mapping all 17 LiveKit DisconnectReason enum values (UNKNOWN_REASON, CLIENT_INITIATED, DUPLICATE_IDENTITY, SERVER_SHUTDOWN, PARTICIPANT_REMOVED, ROOM_DELETED, STATE_MISMATCH, JOIN_FAILURE, MIGRATION, SIGNAL_CLOSE, ROOM_CLOSED, USER_UNAVAILABLE, USER_REJECTED, SIP_TRUNK_FAILURE, CONNECTION_TIMEOUT, MEDIA_FAILURE, AGENT_ERROR) + `undefined` to one of 6 hypothesis tags: `client-cleanup` | `duplicate-identity` | `transport-failure` | `media-failure` | `server-initiated` | `unknown`. Each diagnosis carries a short description pointing to the suspected root cause + the diagnostic next-step. Helper is pure (no I/O, no React), matches established `end-of-call-policy.ts` / `guest-bypass.ts` / `guest-credentials.ts` pattern so it's testable in vitest's node environment without jsdom/RTL. (b) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/livekit/disconnect-reason.test.ts` (+112) — 9 RED→GREEN tests. Coverage: CLIENT_INITIATED → client-cleanup with cleanup/hangup language; DUPLICATE_IDENTITY → duplicate-identity with StrictMode language; CONNECTION_TIMEOUT → transport-failure with ICE/TURN/coturn language; SIGNAL_CLOSE → transport-failure; JOIN_FAILURE → transport-failure; MEDIA_FAILURE → media-failure; SERVER_SHUTDOWN/ROOM_DELETED/PARTICIPANT_REMOVED → server-initiated; UNKNOWN_REASON + undefined → unknown hypothesis with consistent label; exhaustive enum sweep proving every value yields a non-empty description + label. RED proven by first vitest run failing with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND before the module existed; GREEN 9/9 after impl. (c) MODIFIED `apps/web/src/lib/livekit/use-meeting-room.ts` (+15/-2) — added `type DisconnectReason` type-only import (lint required mixed-mode `import { Room, RoomEvent, type DisconnectReason }`), `describeDisconnectReason` value import. RoomEvent.Disconnected handler now receives `(reason?: DisconnectReason)`, computes diagnosis, console.warn's a structured one-line log `[livekit] RoomEvent.Disconnected — reason={LABEL} hypothesis={HYPOTHESIS} :: {description}` BEFORE flipping status to "ended". Pure instrumentation — the status flip remains identical; status="ended" still fires; downstream consumers see no behavioral change. VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 246/246 ✓ (was 237 prior, +9 helper); pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (after fixing initial `consistent-type-imports` error on the DisconnectReason value-vs-type import; 2 pre-existing warnings outside diff unchanged); pnpm typecheck ✓ clean; pnpm build ✓ 22 routes; **middleware bundle 141 kB UNCHANGED** (Edge surface guard per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] — `use-meeting-room.ts` is not transitively imported by middleware/instrumentation, but mandatory build also rules out indirect graph regression). TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — STATE.md NEXT scope met (DisconnectReason now captured at the canonical fire site, hypothesis derivation baked into code so smoke test output is self-interpreting). Stage 2 quality PASS — TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (vitest first run = ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND; vitest second run after impl = 9/9 passed); zero `any` types added; scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan; no scope creep (resisted the temptation to ALSO ship the loader memo fix — that stays its own ticket `(guest-meeting-loader-memo-stability)` per memory); conventional commit ahead `dbaa90c`. SECURITY (security.md compliance): console.warn diagnostic does not leak credentials/PII (logs only enum label + hypothesis tag + canned description text); no token, no JWT identity, no session data, no user input echoed. SAFE FOR PRODUCTION. HYPOTHESIS (c) CONFIRMED BY INSPECTION (not yet by smoke): `guest-meeting-room-loader.tsx:91-94` creates fresh `{livekitJwt, wsUrl}` object literal every render; `use-meeting-room.ts:151` effect dep array includes `guestCredentials` → parent re-render = effect re-run = cleanup `room.disconnect()` = expected DisconnectReason CLIENT_INITIATED → status="ended". This is the prima facie case for (c). However per Rule 29 (no fuzzy reasoning) the SMOKE TEST remains the required step before declaring root cause — code inspection ≠ runtime observation, and (a) coturn issues could ALSO present as CLIENT_INITIATED if a network-level failure triggers client-side reconnect-then-disconnect logic. BROWSER SMOKE: RAN 2026-05-21 16:17 — RESULT INITIALLY MIS-INTERPRETED, CORRECTED 2026-05-21 16:55 BY FOLLOW-UP FIX TICKET. SEE `[[livekit-client-initiated-dual-meaning]]` LESSON. After the initial squash-merge `c585e52`, user prompted continuation of the smoke task. Sequence: started `pnpm dev` background (Next dev on default port 3000 — APP_PORT env not loaded into shell, harmless; dev ready in 2.7s); confirmed test meeting `cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6` with token `a495c303-3d1a-47f7-b99c-a055319f6b74` still `active` in dev DB; opened Playwright MCP (system Chrome), evaluated `document.cookie` + sessionStorage = empty (truly sessionless context, no host pollution per [[guest-meeting-browser-smoke-2026-05-20]] precedent), navigated `/join/{token}`, filled name "Smoke-DisconnectReason", clicked "Join meeting" (Turnstile test-key auto-passed server-side), redirected to `/app/meeting/cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6?guest=1`, waited 8s for the documented ~3s post-signal-connect disconnect window. Captured the instrumented console.warn: **`[livekit] RoomEvent.Disconnected — reason=CLIENT_INITIATED hypothesis=client-cleanup :: Our own code called room.disconnect(). Likely a useEffect cleanup or hangup() — check effect deps for unstable refs.`** plus LiveKit's own diagnostic line `Abort connection attempt due to user initiated disconnect` just before that. **HYPOTHESIS (c) CONFIRMED EMPIRICALLY** — matches the code-inspection prediction. Hypothesis (a) transport-failure RULED OUT (would have produced CONNECTION_TIMEOUT / SIGNAL_CLOSE / JOIN_FAILURE / MEDIA_FAILURE label, not CLIENT_INITIATED). Hypothesis (b) duplicate-identity RULED OUT (would have produced DUPLICATE_IDENTITY label). The instrumentation worked exactly as designed — the hypothesis tag in the output IS the verdict, the description points directly at the fix. 0 NEW TYPED LESSONS — instrumentation is a known pattern; no novel gotchas surfaced. SKIPPED next-forge / react-best-practices / nextjs skill auto-suggestions per Rule 28 (skill packs never override governance scope; Yelli is Spec-Driven V31 on Next.js 15). END STATE: every future LiveKit disconnect in the codebase now console.warn's a structured diagnosis at warn-level (visible in default DevTools filter). Closes the INSTRUMENTATION phase of `(guest-meeting-livekit-peer-disconnect)`; OBSERVATION phase queued.

PRIOR_PHASE_2: (meeting-room-guest-disconnect-redirect) — COMPLETE 2026-05-21, squash-merged to main as `24101c6` (implementation branch was `feat/meeting-room-guest-disconnect-redirect`, branched from main tip `57f1d63`, intermediate commits `39599a3` source + `b50a726` governance, branch deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 files / 148 insertions / 8 deletions). SCOPE: Tier 1 follow-up to (guest-meeting-layout-bypass) `b1b6391`. Browser smoke at end of that ticket showed sessionless guests reaching the meeting page AND LiveKit signal-connecting, then `MeetingRoom`'s navigation `useEffect` at meeting-room.tsx:226-230 auto-routing them to `/app/meetings` on `status === "ended"` — which 302s to `/login` because `/app/meetings` is auth-only. INVESTIGATION FIRST (per STATE NEXT block + systematic-debugging skill): traced the disconnect chain — `useMeetingRoom` registers `RoomEvent.Disconnected` at use-meeting-room.ts:114 which calls `setStatus("ended")`. The underlying LiveKit `connecting → disconnected` after ~3s of signal connect is the natural ICE candidate gathering window; hosts on the same hook work fine per Phase 7 #14, so this is guest-path-specific. Three hypotheses ranked: (a) real coturn/ICE/TURN failure on guest path despite `75ab34f` container fix — MOST LIKELY given the ~3s timing; (b) LiveKit duplicate-participant collision from StrictMode double-mount with same JWT identity; (c) `useMeetingRoom` effect re-running due to unstable `guestCredentials` object reference from `guest-meeting-room-loader.tsx:91-94` (fresh object literal every render). Concluded: the disconnect root cause is a SEPARATE bug — file as `(guest-meeting-livekit-peer-disconnect)` and ship the redirect fix now so guests don't bounce to /login regardless of disconnect cause. FIX (3 files, 2 new, 1 modified): (a) `apps/web/src/components/meeting/end-of-call-policy.ts` (+44) — NEW pure helper `endOfCallPolicy({ isGuest }): { redirectAfterEnded, endedMessage, failedCtaHref, failedCtaLabel }`. Host: `/app/meetings` + "Redirecting…" (byte-for-byte match prior host UX). Guest: `null` redirect + "Meeting ended. Thanks for joining." + `/` failed CTA. Pure-function extraction matches `guest-bypass.ts` + `guest-credentials.ts` pattern (vitest env is node, no jsdom/RTL configured). (b) `apps/web/src/components/meeting/end-of-call-policy.test.ts` (+88) — 8 RED→GREEN tests. RED step proved with stub returning `"STUB"` for all fields: 5 failed | 3 passed-trivially. GREEN after real impl: 8/8 passed. (c) `apps/web/src/components/meeting/meeting-room.tsx` (+14 / -8) — import policy helper, compute `policy = endOfCallPolicy({ isGuest: guestCredentials !== undefined })` after `useMeetingRoom` hook, gated three sites: (1) the `status === "ended"` `useEffect` now checks `policy.redirectAfterEnded !== null` before calling `router.replace(target)`; (2) the failed-state CTA button uses `policy.failedCtaHref` + `policy.failedCtaLabel`; (3) the ended-status render uses `policy.endedMessage`. Effect dep updated to include `policy.redirectAfterEnded` (string|null — stable by value). VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 237/237 ✓ (was 229, +8 new); pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff unchanged); pnpm typecheck ✓ clean; pnpm build ✓ 22 routes; **middleware bundle 141 kB UNCHANGED** (Edge surface guard per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]]). TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — STATE.md NEXT scope met: navigation gated on `guestCredentials !== undefined` (axis is `isGuest`, NOT `isHost` — authed-non-host participants still have session + `/app/meetings` access); investigation done first; root cause delegated to new ticket; host path byte-for-byte unchanged. Stage 2 quality PASS — TDD RED→GREEN evidenced via stub-first approach (5 failed | 3 passed → 8 passed); zero `any` types; scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan; no scope creep; conventional commit ahead. SECURITY (security.md compliance): guest ended-message is generic — identical copy regardless of disconnect cause (host ended, network drop, token expiry, LiveKit error) per §AUTH DEFAULTS — no information leak; no protected tRPC procedures called on disconnect; `isGuest` derived from prop-driven `guestCredentials` (server-shaped) not from URL or client state. LOADER MEMOIZATION DEFERRED: the unstable-reference issue in `guest-meeting-room-loader.tsx:91-94` (fresh `{livekitJwt, wsUrl}` object literal each render) is a real defense-in-depth concern but has no TDD path in node-only vitest env (would need RTL + jsdom which aren't configured). Filed as `(guest-meeting-loader-memo-stability)` per strict TDD iron law adherence. 0 NEW TYPED LESSONS — no novel surface; established pure-helper pattern + standard Rule-25 TDD. SKIPPED next-forge / react-best-practices / nextjs skill auto-suggestions per Rule 28 (skill packs never override governance scope; Yelli is Spec-Driven V31 on Next.js 15, not next-forge migration). END STATE: sessionless guest now stays on `/app/meeting/{id}?guest=1` after LiveKit disconnect, sees "Meeting ended. Thanks for joining." Closes `(meeting-room-guest-disconnect-redirect)` from queue.

PRIOR_PHASE: (guest-meeting-layout-bypass) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `b1b6391` (implementation branch was `655d769`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 4 files / 113 insertions / 2 deletions, all small + atomic + within blast radius). SCOPE: from (guest-meeting-browser-smoke) [[guest-meeting-layout-bypass-missing]] — fix the 4th gate missed by (guest-meeting-page-render) `ff5d356`. `apps/web/src/app/app/layout.tsx:14` does its OWN `if (!session?.user) redirect("/login")` which fires BEFORE the page-level `guest=1` branch in Next.js App Router server-rendering order — sessionless guests hit /login regardless of the middleware bypass. FIX (4 files, 0 new files, 4 modified): (a) `apps/web/src/server/guest-bypass.ts` (+18) — NEW exports `GUEST_BYPASS_HEADER` constant (`"x-yelli-guest-bypass"`) + NEW pure helper `isGuestBypassFromHeaders(Headers): boolean` (strict equality on `=== "1"`, no fuzzy truthiness — never accepts `"true"`/`"on"`/empty/whitespace). Header name is project-namespaced to avoid collision with platform headers (`x-vercel-*`, `x-fwd-*`). (b) `apps/web/src/server/guest-bypass.test.ts` (+65) — 6 RED→GREEN tests (header present `"1"` → true; absent → false; empty string → false; truthy-looking-but-not-"1" → false using Headers-normalized values `"true" "yes" "0" "on" "TRUE" "11" "01"`; case-insensitive header name lookup per Fetch spec → true; similar-named header `"x-yelli-guest-bypass-fake"` → false). All 6 confirmed RED before implementation; all 6 GREEN after. (c) `apps/web/src/middleware.ts` (+15/-2) — added `GUEST_BYPASS_HEADER` import; when `isGuestBypass === true`, calls `requestHeaders.set(GUEST_BYPASS_HEADER, "1")` which propagates to downstream Server Components via both `NextResponse.next({ request: { headers: requestHeaders } })` AND `NextResponse.rewrite(...)` paths. Header set is AFTER auth/tenant cross-checks complete, so guests don't pollute those checks. (d) `apps/web/src/app/app/layout.tsx` (+17) — added `isGuestBypassFromHeaders` + `headers` imports; reads `await headers()` server-side BEFORE the `auth()` call; if bypass → returns `<div className="min-h-screen bg-background">{children}</div>` directly (no auth check, no `<SocketProvider>` wrap, no `<IncomingCallDialog />`). Else existing flow unchanged (auth() → redirect("/login") if no session → wrap in SocketProvider + IncomingCallDialog for hosts). VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 229/229 ✓ (was 223, +6 new); pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff unchanged); pnpm typecheck ✓ clean; pnpm build ✓ 22 routes; **middleware bundle 141 kB UNCHANGED** (MANDATORY Edge surface guard per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] — the +15 lines in middleware.ts were pure logic inside the existing function, no new imports that bloat the Edge bundle). TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — STATE.md NEXT scope met (sessionless guest can GET `/app/meeting/{id}?guest=1` and see MeetingRoom render + LiveKit websocket connect as guest-prefixed participant — verified end-to-end in browser smoke); host-authed path unchanged (no behavioral regression). Stage 2 quality PASS — TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (6 tests failed BEFORE helper existed, all passed after — vitest run captures both states), zero `any` types added, scope = exactly 4 files matching pre-declared plan, no scope creep, conventional commit ahead, defense-in-depth posture maintained (header set is narrow — strict equality, project-namespaced, only set when middleware's narrow bypass check passes; layout reads header via strict equality; LiveKit JWT remains the cryptographic credential). BROWSER SMOKE: PASS — full end-to-end sessionless guest path verified via Playwright MCP. Flow: clean context (no cookies, empty sessionStorage) → GET `/join/{token}` → 200 with join form rendered → fill name "Smoke Guest v2" → submit (Turnstile test-key auto-passes server-side) → tRPC `meetings.exchangeGuestToken` succeeded (elapsed 7381ms — first-call cold start) → sessionStorage populated under `yelli:guest-meeting:cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6` → router pushed to `/app/meeting/{id}?guest=1` → URL persisted (no redirect to /login — layout bypass working) → page title "Meeting — Yelli · Yelli" (the defensive Rule 0 placeholder, NOT the real meeting title — confirms guest path active) → GuestMeetingRoomLoader read sessionStorage + mounted `<MeetingRoom>` → LiveKit signal-connected to `ws://localhost:43532/rtc/v1?access_token=...` → server returned `connected to Livekit Server edition: 0, version: 1.11.0, protocol: 17, region: , nodeId: ND_6jzVXftRoCcz` with `room: meeting-866db757-2cb9-4d21-9b94-1bcf801a379e`, `participant: guest-cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6-1779281088770`. SECONDARY DEFECT FOUND (out of scope, queued): after LiveKit's `connected to Livekit Server`, the state machine transitions `connecting -> disconnected` and the page auto-navigates to `/app/meetings` (which then 302s sessionless guest to `/login?callbackUrl=%2Fapp%2Fmeetings`). Reproducible without hot-reload artifact. Likely cause: `MeetingRoom`/`useMeetingRoom` host-side onDisconnect handler navigates to the meeting list as a "leave call" UX — for hosts this works; for guests, the meeting list is a protected route. STATE.md [[guest-meeting-browser-smoke-2026-05-20]] §guest-flow gotcha (c) already noted "useEmitCallParticipation socket emit will likely fail silently for guests" — this may be related. Queued as `(meeting-room-guest-disconnect-redirect)` Tier 1. 0 NEW TYPED LESSONS this ticket — the secondary defect is documented in this PHASE block + CHANGELOG; a standalone lesson can be added when the fix ticket runs. SKIPPED next-forge / nextjs / routing-middleware skill prompts (Yelli is on Next.js 15 not 16; proxy.ts migration is a separate ticket; Rule 28 priority — skill packs never override governance/scope). END STATE: 4-gate guest meeting path now complete (middleware bypass + sessionStorage parser + LiveKit JWT mint + layout bypass). Sessionless guests can reach the meeting page and connect to LiveKit. Closes `(guest-meeting-layout-bypass)` from queue.

(3) Defensive parser `parseGuestMeetingCredentials` would accept the stored shape (verified field shape matches: livekitJwt/wsUrl/roomName/displayName, no `livekitUrl`). (4) LiveKit JWT minted by `meetings.exchangeGuestToken` is valid — when the layout was permissive (host session polluting), the LiveKit websocket signal-connected to `ws://localhost:43532/rtc/v1?access_token=...` and the server returned `connected to Livekit Server edition: 0, version: 1.11.0, protocol: 17, region: , nodeId: ND_6jzVXftRoCcz` with the correct room name + guest-prefixed participant identity `guest-cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6-1779277145211`. (5) sessionStorage IS independent of auth cookies — survived host sign-out in the same tab. **WHAT THE SMOKE BLOCKED ON**: layout-level redirect to /login fires before page renders for any sessionless request. Reproduced 2× (initial submit-redirect flow + direct re-navigation after sign-out). **SIBLING DISCOVERIES** (not blockers, queued as new tickets): (a) dev compose container is broken for Prisma — `apps/web/Dockerfile` standalone-trace drops the engine binaries because `next.config.ts` lacks `outputFileTracingIncludes` for Prisma; daily dev uses native `pnpm dev` which has no such issue (compose comment at lines 8-11 documents this). Queued as `(meeting-host-copy-join-link)`. (d) STATE.md gotcha (c) CONFIRMED — socket.io fails for guests (`WebSocket closed before connection is established`); per STATE.md instruction not pulled into this session. (e) Playwright MCP browser profile is polluted with webmaster credentials from a prior session (Chrome password manager autofill of `webmaster@yelli.local` + the real seeded password on /login). Not an app defect — test-infra hygiene only. **SESSION ENVIRONMENT STATE**: `yelli_dev_app` container is STOPPED (broken parity image, not restarted); native `pnpm dev` is RUNNING on port 43512 as PID 200281; LiveKit + coturn + postgres + valkey + minio + mailhog + pgbouncer + livekit_egress all UP. **TEST DATA**: `smoke-host-20260520@yelli.test` org `Smoke Test Org` slug `smoketest` exists in dev DB; meeting `cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6` (Guest Smoke Meeting) exists with `meeting_link_token a495c303-3d1a-47f7-b99c-a055319f6b74`. Cleanup deferred to a future housekeeping pass. PHASE_DEMOTED_FROM_PRIOR: (guest-meeting-page-render) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `ff5d356` (implementation branch was `281c14f`, deleted post-squash). (e) MODIFIED `apps/web/src/lib/livekit/use-meeting-room.ts` — added optional `guestCredentials` param; when present, skip the `getJoinToken` protectedProcedure mutation and connect with pre-minted wsUrl+token directly (isHost forced false). (h) MODIFIED `apps/web/src/app/(auth)/join/[token]/page.tsx` — DRY'd local `guestSessionStorageKey()` to import `guestCredentialsStorageKey` from `@/server/guest-credentials`; locks writer/reader contract. (i) TESTS: 8 RED→GREEN for guest-bypass (happy path, missing param, wrong value, wrong path, no id, nested subroute, path traversal, extra params) + 8 RED→GREEN for guest-credentials (happy path, null, empty, malformed JSON, missing field, empty field, non-string field, extra-field stripping with prototype-pollution defense). PHASE_DEMOTED_FROM_PRIOR: (join-token-trpc) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `035ec2a` (implementation branch was `2a1fd30`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 tracked files + 1 governance file / 527 insertions / 14 deletions across the diff). SCOPE: STATE.md (d) ticket — wire the public guest meeting join page (`apps/web/src/app/(auth)/join/[token]/page.tsx`) end-to-end via tRPC. Both prior blockers cleared 2026-05-20 (LiveKit env rename `f9f88bf`, coturn squash `75ab34f`, dev env squash `3677af2`). FIX (3 source files + CHANGELOG): (a) NEW `meetings.exchangeGuestToken` publicProcedure (~115 lines) in `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/meetings.ts`. Input: `{ token, displayName, turnstileToken }` strict Zod schema. Flow: rate-limit by IP via `rateLimiters.public.check(\`guestJoin:${ip}\`)` → `verifyTurnstileToken` server-side → `platformPrisma.meeting.findUnique` by `meeting_link_token` (no L6 — guests have no session context; the token IS the credential, the row carries org_id) → generic NOT_FOUND "Invalid or expired link." for ALL of unknown/cancelled/ended/locked (same message to prevent token enumeration) → mint LiveKit JWT with identity=`guest-${meetingId}-${Date.now()}` → record Participant row with `organization_id` stamped from the looked-up meeting (NEVER from client input) → return `{ meetingId, livekitJwt, wsUrl, roomName }`. Response NEVER contains organization_id (Rule 0). (b) 15 RED→GREEN tests in `meetings.test.ts` + extended `@yelli/db` mock to expose `platformPrisma.meeting.findUnique` + `platformPrisma.participant.create` + new `verifyTurnstileToken` mock. Coverage: happy path, IP-keyed rate-limit, rate-limit-first-no-DB, turnstile failure UNAUTHORIZED, unknown/cancelled/ended/locked all returning same generic NOT_FOUND, LiveKit mint failure SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, Zod min/max on token+displayName, missing turnstileToken, strict() rejection of client-injected organizationId, displayName whitespace trim verification. (c) `apps/web/src/app/(auth)/join/[token]/page.tsx` — replaced `router.push` placeholder with `trpc.meetings.exchangeGuestToken.useMutation` wiring. On success: persists `{ livekitJwt, wsUrl, roomName, displayName }` to sessionStorage under key `yelli:guest-meeting:${meetingId}` (per-tab, ephemeral, 6h server-side JWT cap) then `router.push('/app/meeting/${meetingId}?guest=1')`. On error: resets captcha token (Turnstile is single-use). Submit disabled while `mutation.isPending`. VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 207/207 ✓ (was 192, +15 new for exchangeGuestToken). pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (1 import-order error fixed: `@trpc/server` reordered before `@yelli/db` in meetings.test.ts; 2 pre-existing warnings unchanged outside diff). pnpm typecheck ✓ clean. pnpm build ✓ 22 routes; middleware 141 kB UNCHANGED (no middleware touched); `/join/[token]` route now 7.19 kB / 172 kB first-load (was 4.06 kB / 168 kB — accounts for trpc.useMutation client wiring). TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — STATE.md TDD acceptance criteria met (real tRPC procedure validates token + status guard + locked guard + mint LiveKit + return JWT+room+meetingId; page wires form to procedure). Stage 2 quality PASS — TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (15 tests FAILED with "No procedure found on path 'exchangeGuestToken'" BEFORE procedure was implemented, all PASSED after), zero `any` types added, scope = 3 source files matching pre-declared plan (test + procedure + page wiring), strict Zod with `.strict()`, conventional commit ahead, shadcn primitives only. 0 NEW TYPED LESSONS — straightforward Tier 1 implementation following the established `authRouter.register` publicProcedure pattern + existing `meetingsRouter.getJoinToken` protectedProcedure pattern. No new gotchas surfaced; no architectural decisions made; no security insights beyond existing rules. BROWSER SMOKE: KNOWN PARTIAL — `/app/meeting/[id]?guest=1` currently 302s to /login because `PROTECTED_PREFIXES` in `middleware.ts:22` doesn't accommodate guests. The sessionStorage payload IS ready for the next ticket. This is a clean follow-up boundary, NOT a defect in this ticket — STATE.md scope was "wire the tRPC mutation" + "form bindings", both delivered cleanly. NEW QUEUE ITEMS (5 follow-up tickets logged in CHANGELOG): (guest-meeting-page-render) middleware bypass or public guest route or inline LiveKit render on join page — pick one before browser smoke fully passes; (meeting-token-expiry) Meeting model has no expires_at field — tokens are effectively perpetual until cancel/end; (meeting-token-single-use) tokens are re-usable — could add consumed_at for single-use semantics; (lobby-admit-step) `Meeting.lobby_enabled` flag exists but isn't honored by exchangeGuestToken (lobby admit-by-host flow deferred); (participant-trim-cleanup) `Participant.left_at` never stamped for guest flow — needs websocket/livekit-webhook integration. END STATE: tRPC layer + form binding for guest meeting joins is live; the guest can submit the form, get a real LiveKit JWT, and have their sessionStorage primed for the next-ticket guest meeting page render. Closes (d) join/[token] tRPC from the resume queue.

PHASE_DEMOTED_FROM_PRIOR: (dev-app-redis-url) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `3677af2` (implementation branch was `1fa4cb5`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 1 tracked file / 6 insertions / 1 deletion + 1 gitignored env regen). SCOPE EXPANDED mid-ticket from REDIS_URL-only to REDIS_URL + Prisma binary because the ticket's stated value ("unblock browser smoke for UI work") required fixing BOTH stacked blockers — REDIS_URL alone would have left the app crashing on first DB query. Same in-scope-by-necessity pattern as [[coturn-config-fix]] precedent. ROOT CAUSE BUG A (REDIS_URL): Phase 3 generated REDIS_PASSWORD via `openssl rand -base64 32 | head -c 22` which can include `/+=` (base64 alphabet); the password landed as `Ro2JxvBIBJ/aEkhvALnFB3` containing a literal `/`. `deploy/compose/dev/docker-compose.app.yml:34` constructs `REDIS_URL: redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@${COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME}_valkey:6379` by raw string interpolation — the `/` breaks the URL into user:pass@host + path, failing WHATWG URL parse + Zod's `.url()` validation at instrumentation-hook load. ROOT CAUSE BUG B (Prisma): After clearing Bug A, app crashed on `PrismaClientInitializationError: Prisma Client could not locate the Query Engine for runtime "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"` — host (WSL2 debian/glibc) generates a debian-only Prisma client, Dockerfile uses node:22-alpine (musl), the .prisma/client copied from host build context has the wrong .so.node. FIX (2 changes): (a) regenerated REDIS_PASSWORD in .env.dev (gitignored) via `openssl rand -hex 16` → 32-char URL-safe hex; updated REDIS_URL field in lockstep. Secret values never entered AI context (sed flow with `unset` at end). (b) added `binaryTargets = ["native", "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"]` to `generator client` in packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma; `pnpm db:generate` produced both `libquery_engine-debian-openssl-3.0.x.so.node` AND `libquery_engine-linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x.so.node` in .prisma/client. Prisma picks the right one at runtime per process.platform + libc detection. VALIDATION: `bash deploy/compose/start.sh dev down && up -d` brought stack up healthy (one transient `docker builder prune -f` needed to clear a snapshot-extraction cache corruption during the first up attempt — unrelated to either fix); `docker logs yelli_dev_app` shows `✓ Starting...` + `✓ Ready in 243ms` + no Invalid-server-env crashes + no Prisma engine errors; `curl http://localhost:43512/` → HTTP 200 with 13.6kB HTML; grep of rendered HTML confirmed all 5 expected unauthed-state strings present ("Yelli", "Real-time team comms", "Get started", "Sign in", "Powerbyte") — no "Go to app" (correct, unauthenticated request). pnpm test 192/192 ✓ (unchanged); pnpm typecheck ✓ clean; pnpm build ✓ 22 routes unchanged. TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — ticket goal "unblock browser smoke for UI work" satisfied (HTTP 200 + expected landing content rendered + Prisma client loads cleanly); scope expansion was in-scope-by-necessity per [[coturn-config-fix]] precedent. Stage 2 quality PASS — single-line schema edit + URL-safe password regen, no `any` types added, no scope creep beyond what's needed to boot the app, conventional commit ahead. 1 NEW TYPED LESSON: ⚖️ [[url-unsafe-chars-in-interpolated-passwords]] documenting (a) base64 `/+=` × inline-compose-URL-interpolation footgun, (b) recovery pattern (URL-safe hex regen), (c) Phase 3 generation policy gap (passwords feeding URLs must be URL-safe — either openssl rand -hex, or strip `/+=` from base64), (d) Prisma musl binary as a closely-related Alpine deployment gotcha, (e) STACKED-BUG RULE codifying when to expand a ticket's scope (small + masked + delivery requires both). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — defect fix, not architectural decision (though the future Phase 3 policy IS a candidate for a follow-up DECISIONS_LOG entry once implemented). BONUS SIDE-EFFECT: this session also confirmed the previous (root-landing-page) ticket renders correctly in the browser — that ticket's smoke was blocked then, now done. NEW QUEUE ITEMS (NOT fixed this ticket): (a) Phase 3 password generation spec — mandate URL-safe characters for any password that feeds into a URL via raw compose interpolation; affects future projects bootstrapped from this framework. (b) Compose interpolation pattern — generalize to reference `${REDIS_URL_DOCKER}` from .env.* (where URL encoding is explicit + verifiable) instead of constructing inline; touches 9 compose files across dev/stage/prod for redis/postgres/etc. (c) Staging/prod env audit — gitignored, can't verify from here, but the same /+= class issue likely affects them; recommend fresh URL-safe regen during next deploy. SKIPPED bootstrap + vercel-storage skill suggestions — Prisma binaryTargets is standard Prisma API, not Vercel-specific. END STATE: dev environment fully restored; browser smoke now reliable for all UI tickets going forward; Phase 7 UI loop unblocked. Closes (dev-app-redis-url) from queue. Squash-merge pending.

PHASE_PRIOR: (root-landing-page) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `4c64b2d` (implementation branch was `ee571b8`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 files / ~118 net insertions). User selected "Minimal stub" + "Show landing + Go-to-app CTA" via AskUserQuestion before any code. SYMPTOM: prior `apps/web/src/app/page.tsx` was a 10-line auth-redirect (self-hosted style — `if (session?.user) redirect("/app"); else redirect("/login")`); never matched PRODUCT.md line 233 (`/` = "Public landing page (SaaS) or login redirect (self-hosted)"). Yelli is the SaaS deployment, so a real public landing was the correct surface. FIX (3 files): (a) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/landing/cta.ts` (~28 lines) — pure helper `getLandingCTAs({ isAuthed }): { primary, secondary? }` returning plain-data CTA descriptors (no functions, no JSX → safe for Server→Client serialization if extended later). Authed: `{ primary: { href:"/app", label:"Go to app" } }` only. Unauthed: `{ primary: { href:"/register", label:"Get started" }, secondary: { href:"/login", label:"Sign in" } }`. (b) NEW `apps/web/src/lib/landing/cta.test.ts` (~50 lines, 6 RED→GREEN) — happy paths + plain-data shape + funnel-priority guards (locks primary=/register-not-/login for unauthed; locks authed primary=/app-not-/login). (c) REPLACED page.tsx with public landing — Server Component, awaits `auth()` once, calls `getLandingCTAs({ isAuthed: Boolean(session?.user) })`, renders header (Yelli wordmark + nav with secondary + primary CTAs) + hero (h1 + lede + duplicated CTAs as primary actions) + thin footer (© 2026 Powerbyte). Uses `<Button asChild>` + `<Link>` from `@yelli/ui/button` + `next/link` per established login-page convention. Tailwind utility classes only; shadcn/ui CSS variables for color tokens (Rule 21 graceful degradation — no design-system/MASTER.md present, falls back to shadcn neutral defaults). VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 192/192 ✓ (was 186, +6 new for getLandingCTAs); pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff unchanged: app/layout.tsx no-css-tags + calls.test.ts non-null assertion); pnpm typecheck ✓ clean; pnpm build ✓ 22 routes + middleware 141kB unchanged + `/` route now ƒ 1.75kB / 115kB first-load (was a dynamic redirect with same SSR posture — now serves HTML instead of issuing 307). BROWSER SMOKE: BLOCKED on PRE-EXISTING dev infra issue — `yelli_dev_app` container was already in `Up 2h (unhealthy)` state before this session began, failing instrumentation hook with `Invalid server environment variables: REDIS_URL: Invalid url`. ALL routes (/, /login, /api/health) currently return HTTP 500 from the container — not from my landing change (the container is running a stale build that pre-dates my code AND the env validation crashes BEFORE any route handler is invoked). Risk-mitigated for sign-off by: (a) `pnpm build` compilation success, (b) full unit coverage of the only branching logic (CTA selection — 6 RED→GREEN), (c) Server Component with NO client interactivity / NO client-side data fetching, (d) Tailwind utility classes only (no custom CSS / no new dependencies), (e) `<Button asChild>` + Link pattern already battle-tested across /login, /register, /forgot-password. TWO-STAGE REVIEW (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec PASS — PRODUCT.md `/` is Public + matches user-confirmed scope (hero + two CTAs + footer + authed-show-Go-to-app over force-redirect, Vercel/Stripe pattern); Stage 2 quality PASS — zero `any` types added; TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (6 tests failed first then all passed after implementation); scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan (helper + test + page); no scope creep — no marketing depth, no shadcnblocks pulled in, no new packages; conventional commit message ahead; shadcn primitives only (UI Rule #1). 1 NEW TYPED LESSON: 🔴 [[dev-app-redis-url-invalid]] documenting the `Up (unhealthy)` + uniform-HTTP-500 + instrumentation-crash pattern and the `docker logs ... | grep -B2 -A8 "Invalid server"` recovery diagnostic. 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — feature addition, not architectural decision. NEW QUEUE FINDING (NOT fixed in this ticket): (dev-app-redis-url) — yelli_dev_app instrumentation hook fails with REDIS_URL: Invalid url; likely .env.dev value clobbered by an unrelated session OR env.ts schema tightened beyond the .env.dev format. Recovery path: compare .env.dev REDIS_URL against env.ts Zod schema, fix the value or relax the schema, restart container. SKIPPED next-cache-components skill — out of scope for a 10-line redirect → minimal stub conversion. END STATE: `/` now serves the SaaS public landing for unauthed visitors and offers "Go to app" for authed. Closes (f) Root landing page from Phase 7 #17 backlog. Squash-merge pending.

PHASE_PRIOR: (t-slug-dev-routes-broken) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged to main as `4142f79` (implementation branch was `038ebe7`, deleted post-squash). Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller execution (no Sonnet dispatch — 3 files / 146 insertions / 8 deletions). Symptom: `/t/{slug}/<anything>` always 404'd on localhost dev. Root cause: middleware.ts extracted `tenantSlug` correctly into `x-tenant-slug` header but NEVER stripped the `/t/{slug}` prefix from the path → Next.js route resolution searched for a non-existent `/t/[slug]/app/...` handler tree. Bug was invisible to existing tests because `buildTenantRedirectUrl` tests only assert URL strings, never serve a request through the middleware → route stack. FIX (3 files): (a) NEW pure helper `stripTenantPathPrefix(path, slug)` in `apps/web/src/server/tenant-redirect.ts` (~25 lines — uses `path.match()` with regex-escaped slug; returns canonical path or original on no-match; defensive against regex-special-char slugs even though upstream validates `[a-z0-9-]+`). (b) 13 new RED→GREEN tests in `tenant-redirect.test.ts` covering happy path + single segment + no-trailing → "/" + /-trailing → "/" + admin/superadmin/api paths + no-/t/ no-op + empty-slug no-op + slug-mismatch no-op + /tenants/* non-match + /t/yelli-other non-match + regex-special-char defensive. Total file: 26 tests. (c) middleware.ts wiring: compute `effectivePath = stripTenantPathPrefix(path, tenantSlug ?? "")` ONCE; use it for `isProtected` (fixes silent pass-through on unauthenticated /t/yelli/app/foo), `resolveTenantRedirect.path` (fixes /superadmin bypass not matching dev URLs), and the rewrite call itself. When `wasPathStripped`, return `NextResponse.rewrite(rewriteUrl, { request: { headers: requestHeaders } })` so existing /app, /admin, /superadmin handlers serve the request. Headers (x-tenant-slug + x-user-id + x-organization-id + x-organization-slug) DO survive `NextResponse.rewrite` when passed via the `{ request: { headers } }` option. `callbackUrl` on the unauthenticated-redirect path preserves the ORIGINAL `/t/{slug}/...` so login lands the user back on the same tenant-prefixed URL. VALIDATION: pnpm vitest run 186/186 ✓ (was 173, +13 new); pnpm typecheck ✓ 0 errors; pnpm lint ✓ 0 errors (2 pre-existing warnings outside diff: app/layout.tsx no-css-tags + calls.test.ts non-null assertion — both predate this ticket); pnpm build ✓ 22 routes + middleware 141kB UNCHANGED (MANDATORY per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] — middleware.ts is THE Edge surface). Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec compliance PASS — all 4 path families verified (/t/yelli/app, /admin, /superadmin, /api/*) rewrite to canonical paths; headers preserved; tenant cross-check uses effective path so /superadmin bypass works on dev URLs; auth callback URL preserves original /t/{slug}/... prefix; Stage 2 quality PASS — zero `any` types added; TDD RED→GREEN evidenced (13 tests failed first then passed after implementation); scope = exactly 3 files matching pre-declared plan (helper, test, middleware); no scope creep; conventional commit message ahead. SKIPPED routing-middleware skill's `proxy.ts` rename suggestion — out of scope (Next.js 16 migration is a separate ticket; project is on 15). 1 new typed lesson: 🟡 [[t-slug-dev-routes-broken]] documenting the "middleware extracts BUT doesn't rewrite → pure tests pass but 404 in browser" anti-pattern + heuristic ("if pure-function tests pass but the route still 404s, the integration layer is the gap — pure tests cover decision logic, NOT request serving"). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — defect fix, not architectural decision. END STATE: /t/{slug}/* dev URL pattern now works end-to-end. (#7c) subdomain redirect smoke is unblocked from the path-pattern dev URL side; still subject to having multi-user rig for the actual cross-tenant redirect scenarios per [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]].

PHASE_PRIOR: (coturn-config-fix) — COMPLETE 2026-05-20, squash-merged 75ab34f. Tier 1 inline Opus 4.7 controller execution (no Sonnet dispatch — total diff is 3 YAML lines removed across dev/stage/prod docker-compose.media.yml + 3 env values filled in gitignored .env.dev/.env.staging/.env.prod + 1 new section added to gitignored CREDENTIALS.md; nowhere near subagent dispatch threshold). TWO LATENT BUGS were jointly blocking coturn (not just one as STATE.md previously suggested): (a) `COTURN_STATIC_AUTH_SECRET` in .env.dev was the literal placeholder string `devturnsecret_replace_with_real_value` — coturn accepts it (no length validation on the HMAC secret) so it didn't cause the crash directly, but it was a real security hole: anyone with .env.example access could forge dev TURN credentials. (b) `coturn/coturn:latest` image dropped the `--no-tlsv1_1` command-line flag — modern coturn uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` instead of the legacy deny-list `--no-tlsv1_X` scheme. The container exited 255 the moment compose re-parsed command-line args (e.g. on `docker compose restart coturn`) with `turnserver: unrecognized option '--no-tlsv1_1'` on lines 1-2 of the full log, then dumped 135k lines of `-h` help-text. Critical diagnostic discovery: `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text — must use `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` for coturn flag crashes. Bug A alone was a latent vulnerability; bug B was the active crash. Both required fixing in this ticket — neither fix alone would unblock WebRTC. FIX: removed `      - --no-tlsv1_1` line from all 3 compose files via `sed -i '/^      - --no-tlsv1_1$/d'` (modern coturn already disables TLS 1.0/1.1 by default — removal is functionally equivalent to the legacy behavior); generated 3 independent 48-char base64-stripped openssl values (DEV / STAGING / PROD), patched all 3 env files in-place via sed (verified length=48 / all 3 unique / placeholder string count=0 after); added new `## 🔁 Coturn (WebRTC TURN/STUN Server) ✅ FILLED` section to CREDENTIALS.md before the "Where Each File Lives" anchor, documenting per-env realm + length + port + generation command + rotation guidance. Secret values never entered AI context (sed-only shell flow with vars `unset` at end). VALIDATION: `bash deploy/compose/start.sh dev up -d` brought the full stack up cleanly (first attempt with `docker compose up -d coturn --force-recreate` against standalone media.yml failed with `service "livekit" depends on undefined service "valkey": invalid compose project` — media.yml's livekit references valkey via depends_on, requires cache.yml chained); STUN protocol-level test via `docker run --rm --network host coturn/coturn:latest turnutils_stunclient -p 43542 127.0.0.1` returned `IPv4. UDP reflexive addr: 172.25.0.1:49796` (PASS); container `status=running, restarts=0, exitCode=0` stable for 30+ seconds; ports verified mapped 43542→3478 on TCP and UDP, IPv4 and IPv6. No source-code changes → skipped pnpm typecheck/lint/test/build per blast-radius minimum (YAML-only diff, no TypeScript imports touched anywhere); validated via direct functional STUN test instead. Two-stage review (Rule 25): Stage 1 spec compliance PASS — original ticket ask "fill credential, restart container" satisfied AND the deprecated-flag fix was in-scope-by-necessity (without it, credential fix accomplishes nothing); Stage 2 quality PASS — 3-line YAML scope, zero source-code changes, secret values never entered AI context, conventional commit message ahead. 1 new typed lesson logged: 🟡 [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] documenting (a) the flag-rename pattern (deny-list → minimum-version), (b) the critical `docker logs | head` diagnostic, (c) the recognition heuristic ("when coturn restart-loops with empty `docker logs --tail`, ALWAYS check `head` — the actual error is on line 1-2 but coturn writes ~135k lines of help-text after"). 0 new DECISIONS_LOG entries — defect fix, not architectural decision. END STATE: coturn live and serving STUN/TURN on port 43542; (coturn-config-fix) ticket CLOSED. WebRTC peer connection now functional on localhost — meeting #3 from Rule 16 cleanup (which previously PARTIAL'd because "WebRTC peer connection fails on coturn restarting 255") is unblocked. (rule-16-followup-multi-user) — e2e re-smoke for #14/#15/#16 — is no longer blocked from the coturn dimension; still needs multi-browser rig per [[rule-16-cleanup-2026-05-19]].

(b) DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH — added explicit `where: { organization_id: ctx.organizationId }` to every list/count/aggregate query across 7 routers (admin/calls/meetings/departments/chat/recordings/billing). 6 new cross-org isolation tests on admin.test.ts (now 173/173 total). Squash-merged to main as `ab711da` + governance record `ddcbcad`. 1 new typed lesson: 🔴 [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]].

PRIOR_NEXT — (guest-meeting-livekit-peer-disconnect) — DISCHARGED 2026-05-21.
  EVIDENCE FROM PRIOR SESSION: Playwright smoke logged LiveKit signal-connecting (`connected to Livekit Server edition: 0, version: 1.11.0, protocol: 17, region: , nodeId: ND_6jzVXftRoCcz`), then ~3s later state machine transitioned `connecting → disconnected` WITHOUT ever promoting to `connected`. Hosts on the same `useMeetingRoom` hook work fine — so this is guest-path-specific. The ~3s window matches ICE candidate gathering timeout.
  HYPOTHESES RANKED (highest probability first):
    (a) Real coturn/ICE/TURN failure on guest path. Coturn container was restored in `75ab34f` (no longer restart-looping) but actual STUN/TURN media negotiation may still be broken. Most likely given the 3s timing.
    (b) LiveKit server kicks duplicate participant when StrictMode double-mounts and both rooms try to join with the SAME JWT identity (`guest-{meetingId}-{Date.now()}` is minted ONCE in exchangeGuestToken; both StrictMode mounts use the same token → server treats second as displaced first). React 19 + Next 15 + `reactStrictMode: true` confirmed in next.config.
    (c) `useMeetingRoom` effect re-runs because `guestCredentials` object reference is unstable — `apps/web/src/components/meeting/guest-meeting-room-loader.tsx:91-94` creates a fresh `{ livekitJwt, wsUrl }` object literal every render, and the effect dep at use-meeting-room.ts:151 includes that object. Defense-in-depth fix filed as `(guest-meeting-loader-memo-stability)` but that's a different ticket.
  WHAT TO DO:
    1. Add browser-side `RoomEvent.Disconnected` listener that captures the LiveKit `DisconnectReason` enum value (e.g. DUPLICATE_IDENTITY, CLIENT_INITIATED, ROOM_CLOSED, SERVER_SHUTDOWN, JOIN_FAILURE, MIGRATION, SIGNAL_CLOSE, ROOM_DELETED, USER_UNAVAILABLE, USER_REJECTED, JOIN_FAILURE, NEGOTIATION_FAILED). Log it via `console.warn` or via the existing `useMeetingRoom` errorMessage path.
    2. Run a Playwright smoke against `pnpm dev` (port 43512) on the guest happy path. Capture the DisconnectReason. THIS NAILS THE ROOT CAUSE.
    3. Based on reason code:
       - DUPLICATE_IDENTITY → confirms hypothesis (b). Fix: deduplicate the join attempt at the React layer (`useMeetingRoom` strict-mode safety — track if a connect is already in flight via a useRef; OR move the Room construction out of useEffect into a useMemo + useEffect connect with `if (cancelled) room.disconnect()` rather than letting cleanup race).
       - JOIN_FAILURE / NEGOTIATION_FAILED / SIGNAL_CLOSE / no ICE candidates → confirms hypothesis (a). Fix: coturn config (re-check tlsv1.2+ args, TURN realm, port range, public-ip flag, NAT discovery). May involve LiveKit server config side too (TURN_USE_EXTERNAL_IP or LiveKit `turn:` config).
       - CLIENT_INITIATED → confirms hypothesis (c). Fix: see `(guest-meeting-loader-memo-stability)`.
    4. TDD per Rule 25: write failing unit test for the DisconnectReason capture before instrumenting code. The test target is likely `useMeetingRoom` itself — extract the disconnect handler into a pure function `mapDisconnectReason(reason): { userMessage: string; severity: 'transient'|'fatal' }` testable in node env.
  PRE-FLIGHT:
    1. Branch: `git checkout -b feat/guest-meeting-livekit-peer-disconnect` from main (after this session's squash-merge of `feat/meeting-room-guest-disconnect-redirect`).
    2. Re-read STATE.md PRIOR_PHASE + lessons.md 🔴 — especially [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] and [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]].
    3. Native `pnpm dev` (port 43512). Dev compose container is broken (Prisma trace) per [[guest-meeting-browser-smoke-2026-05-20]] §1.
    4. Re-use existing test data: smoke-host-20260520@yelli.test / smoketest org / meeting cmpdzm5ce0005wni4us29b9m6 / token a495c303-3d1a-47f7-b99c-a055319f6b74. Generate a fresh `meeting_link_token` if the prior one was consumed.
  SECURITY GUARDS:
    - Captured DisconnectReason must NOT be displayed verbatim to the user — generic UI per security.md §AUTH DEFAULTS (no enumeration of meeting state).
    - DisconnectReason can be logged to console.warn or a server-side error reporter, but never echoed back to a sessionless guest's DOM.
  OUT OF SCOPE: meeting-token-expiry, meeting-token-single-use, lobby-admit-step, participant-trim-cleanup, dockerfile-prisma-trace-include, dev-app-healthcheck-route, meeting-host-copy-join-link, loader-memo-stability. File NEW tickets for any orthogonal defects found.

    3. Search for existing join-token Prisma model + scaffold: `grep -rn "join_token\|JoinToken\|guest_join\|/join/" packages/db/prisma/ apps/web/src/app/\(auth\)/join/ apps/web/src/server/trpc/` — confirm the table + page scaffold + helper layout.
    4. Search for existing LiveKit mint helper: `grep -rn "mintLiveKitToken\|getJoinToken" apps/web/src/lib/livekit/` — this is the function meetings.getJoinToken already uses; the new public router likely calls the same.
    5. Apply Tiered Decomposition §1: file count + token estimate. Likely Tier 1 (≤4 files: page.tsx wire + new tRPC router/procedure + colocated test + maybe a pure helper for token validation).
  TDD ORDER (Rule 25):
    a. Write failing test for the new tRPC procedure (e.g. `join.validate({ token, displayName }) → { ok, roomName, livekitJwt, wsUrl }` happy path + expired-token + unknown-token + cross-org-leak-guard cases).
    b. Implement procedure under publicProcedure (NOT protectedProcedure — guests have no session). MUST manually verify token belongs to a valid + non-expired join row + return only the org-scoped LiveKit JWT for the specific room. NEVER include organizationId in the response.
    c. Wire page.tsx form submit to call the mutation; on success connect via LiveKit client.
    d. Add display-name + token-expiry edge cases per security.md §INPUT VALIDATION (Zod .strict, bounded display-name length, reject unknown fields).
  SECURITY GUARDS (security.md §INPUT VALIDATION + §AUTH DEFAULTS + §SUPERADMIN):
    - Use a separate `platformPrisma` (no L6) or explicit `where: { token, expires_at: { gt: now } }` lookup — join tokens are inherently org-less at request time (guest, no session) but the row itself encodes the target org. Validate the row, then mint the LiveKit token for ONLY that room. NEVER trust client-sent room name or org id.
    - Rate-limit per IP (rateLimiters.public — 30/min already configured).
    - Generic error responses: "Invalid or expired link." (no enumeration — same message for unknown-token, expired-token, consumed-token).
    - Token single-use OR time-bounded: check existing JoinToken schema for consumed_at / expires_at semantics; if consumed-once semantics are declared, mark consumed atomically inside the validation transaction.
  BROWSER SMOKE: Now reliable. After implementing, generate a test token in DB (seed script or pgAdmin), visit `http://localhost:43512/join/<token>`, fill display name, click join, confirm LiveKit room connects + video+audio enumerate.
  GOTCHAS:
    - LiveKit env var: LIVEKIT_URL (not LIVEKIT_WS_URL) — [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]].
    - Coturn auth secret already valid + WebRTC works on localhost as of `75ab34f`.
    - Any new aggregate/list query MUST include explicit `organization_id` filter — [[l6-super-admin-bypass-leak]] defense-in-depth pattern.
    - Build is MANDATORY per [[instrumentation-edge-stub-required]] if you touch anything transitively imported by middleware or instrumentation hook.
  OUT OF SCOPE (do NOT pull in): turn the join token into a multi-use invite link; add chat to the join page; record the join session; analytics on join attempts. All deferrable.

PRIOR_LESSONS_ADDED (1 prior session): 🟡 [[coturn-no-tlsv1-1-flag-dropped]] — `coturn/coturn:latest` dropped `--no-tlsv1_1` flag (modern image uses minimum-version scheme `--tlsv1_1` / `--no-tlsv1_2` not legacy deny-list). When this flag is passed, turnserver exits 255 and dumps full `-h` help-text — but `docker logs --tail` shows ONLY help-text, the actual error is always on line 1-2. Recognition heuristic for future coturn debugging: `docker logs ... 2>&1 | head` not `tail`. Fix: remove `      - --no-tlsv1_1` from all 3 compose files; modern coturn default already excludes TLS 1.0/1.1 so removal is functionally equivalent. Related: [[livekit-env-name-mismatch]] — both bugs caused dev WebRTC to fail silently, both diagnosed via Rule 16 cleanup pass.

TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 1 — lightweight. Per memory-governance §1: scope was 3 files in 1 module. Score: (3 files × 2.5) + (1 module × 5) + (1 depth × 3) = 15.5. Well under Tier 2 threshold of 25. Inline Opus 4.7 controller was the right call given (a) sequential TDD (write test → run → confirm RED → implement → run → confirm GREEN → wire → validate) is not parallelizable, (b) every step has output that affects the next decision so subagent delegation overhead would exceed savings, (c) the surgical 3-file scope fits comfortably in a single Opus context window. Subagent budget gate (§2.5 / 30K limit) not approached — task estimated ~50K Opus controller total, well under Opus 100K safe execution ceiling.
